title_english: Heart Sūtra of the Perfection of Wisdom
notes: Most widely used Chinese recension. First to use 'Heart' (心) in title. Analytical base text for critical edition following Chinese compositional priority methodology. Now established as a Chinese composition (digest text, chāo jīng 抄經), not a translation from Sanskrit (Nattier 1992, Attwood 2021). The earliest reliably dated reference is a 656 CE letter in which Xuanzang gives Emperor Gāozōng a gold-lettered Heart Sutra (Kotyk 2019). The Fangshan Stele dated 13 March 661 CE is the oldest physical witness (Attwood 2019).
text: 觀自在菩薩，行深般若波羅蜜多時，照見五蘊皆空，度一切苦厄。舍利子，色不異空，空不異色；色即是空，空即是色。受想行識亦復如是。舍利子，是諸法空相，不生不滅，不垢不淨，不增不減。是故空中，無色，無受想行識，無眼耳鼻舌身意，無色聲香味觸法，無眼界，乃至無意識界。無無明，亦無無明盡，乃至無老死，亦無老死盡。無苦集滅道，無智亦無得，以無所得故。菩提薩埵，依般若波羅蜜多故。心無罣礙，無罣礙故。無有恐怖，遠離顚倒夢想，究竟涅槃。三世諸佛，依般若波羅蜜多故，得阿耨多羅三藐三菩提。故知般若波羅蜜多，是大神咒，是大明咒，是無上咒，是無等等咒，能除一切苦，眞實不虛故。說般若波羅蜜多咒，卽說咒曰：揭帝揭帝，波羅揭帝，波羅僧揭帝，菩提僧莎訶。

segments.text: 觀自在菩薩，行深般若波羅蜜多時，照見五蘊皆空，度一切苦厄。
segments.pinyin: Guānzìzài púsà, xíng shēn bōrě bōluómìduō shí, zhàojiàn wǔyùn jiē kōng, dù yīqiè kǔ è.
segments.english_gloss: When Avalokiteśvara Bodhisattva was practicing the profound prajñāpāramitā, he illuminated the five aggregates and saw that they are all empty, thus transcending all suffering.

segments.text: 舍利子，色不異空，空不異色；色即是空，空即是色。受想行識亦復如是。
segments.pinyin: Shèlìzǐ, sè bù yì kōng, kōng bù yì sè; sè jí shì kōng, kōng jí shì sè. Shòu xiǎng xíng shí yì fù rúshì.
segments.english_gloss: Śāriputra, form is not different from emptiness, emptiness is not different from form; form is emptiness, emptiness is form. Feeling, perception, formation, and consciousness are also like this.

segments.text: 舍利子，是諸法空相，不生不滅，不垢不淨，不增不減。
segments.pinyin: Shèlìzǐ, shì zhūfǎ kōng xiāng, bù shēng bù miè, bù gòu bù jìng, bù zēng bù jiǎn.
segments.english_gloss: Śāriputra, the emptiness characteristic of all dharmas is: not arising, not ceasing; not defiled, not pure; not increasing, not decreasing.

segments.text: 是故空中，無色，無受想行識，
segments.pinyin: Shìgù kōng zhōng, wú sè, wú shòu xiǎng xíng shí,
segments.english_gloss: Therefore, in emptiness there is no form, no feeling, perception, formation, or consciousness;

segments.text: 無眼耳鼻舌身意，無色聲香味觸法，
segments.pinyin: wú yǎn ěr bí shé shēn yì, wú sè shēng xiāng wèi chù fǎ,
segments.english_gloss: no eyes, ears, nose, tongue, body, or mind; no forms, sounds, smells, tastes, tangibles, or mental objects;

segments.pinyin: wú yǎn jiè, nǎizhì wú yìshí jiè.
segments.english_gloss: no eye-element, up to and including no mind-consciousness element.

segments.text: 無無明，亦無無明盡，乃至無老死，亦無老死盡。
segments.pinyin: Wú wúmíng, yì wú wúmíng jìn, nǎizhì wú lǎosǐ, yì wú lǎosǐ jìn.
segments.english_gloss: No ignorance and no ending of ignorance, up to and including no old age and death, and no ending of old age and death.

segments.text: 無苦集滅道，無智亦無得，以無所得故。
segments.pinyin: Wú kǔ jí miè dào, wú zhì yì wú dé, yǐ wú suǒ dé gù.
segments.english_gloss: No suffering, origination, cessation, or path; no wisdom and no attainment, because there is nothing to attain.

segments.text: 菩提薩埵，依般若波羅蜜多故。心無罣礙，無罣礙故。無有恐怖，遠離顚倒夢想，究竟涅槃。
segments.pinyin: Pútísàduǒ, yī bōrě bōluómìduō gù. Xīn wú guà'ài, wú guà'ài gù. Wú yǒu kǒngbù, yuǎnlí diāndǎo mèngxiǎng, jiūjìng nièpán.
segments.english_gloss: Because bodhisattvas rely on prajñāpāramitā, their minds are without obstruction. Without obstruction, they have no fear, are far removed from inverted views and dreams, and attain ultimate nirvāṇa.

segments.text: 三世諸佛，依般若波羅蜜多故，得阿耨多羅三藐三菩提。
segments.pinyin: Sānshì zhū fó, yī bōrě bōluómìduō gù, dé ānòuduōluó sānmiǎo sānpútí.
segments.english_gloss: All buddhas of the three times, relying on prajñāpāramitā, attain anuttarā-samyak-saṃbodhi.

segments.text: 故知般若波羅蜜多，是大神咒，是大明咒，是無上咒，是無等等咒，能除一切苦，眞實不虛故。
segments.pinyin: Gùzhī bōrě bōluómìduō, shì dà shén zhòu, shì dà míng zhòu, shì wúshàng zhòu, shì wú děngděng zhòu, néng chú yīqiè kǔ, zhēnshí bù xū gù.
segments.english_gloss: Therefore know that prajñāpāramitā is the great mantra, the mantra of great illumination, the supreme mantra, the unequalled mantra, which can remove all suffering; it is true and not false.

segments.text: 說般若波羅蜜多咒，卽說咒曰：揭帝揭帝，波羅揭帝，波羅僧揭帝，菩提僧莎訶。
segments.pinyin: Shuō bōrě bōluómìduō zhòu, jí shuō zhòu yuē: Jiēdì jiēdì, bōluó jiēdì, bōluó sēng jiēdì, pútí sēng suōhē.
segments.english_gloss: Therefore proclaim the prajñāpāramitā mantra, proclaim the mantra that says: Gate gate pāragate pārasaṃgate bodhi svāhā.

scholarly_refs: Nattier, Jan (1992). 'The Heart Sūtra: a Chinese apocryphal text?' JIABS 15(2): 153-223.
Attwood, Jayarava (2015). 'Heart Murmurs: Some Problems with Conze's Prajñāpāramitāhṛdaya.' JOCBS 8: 28-48.
Attwood, Jayarava (2017b). 'Epithets of the Mantra in the Heart Sutra.' JOCBS 12: 26-57.
Attwood, Jayarava (2019). 'Xuánzàng's Relationship to the Heart Sutra in light of the Fangshan Stele.' Journal of Chinese Buddhist Studies 32: 1-30.
Attwood, Jayarava (2020a). 'Ungarbling Section VI of the Heart Sutra.' JOCBS 18: 11-41.
Attwood, Jayarava (2020c). 'The History of the Heart Sutra as a Palimpsest.' Pacific World Series 4, no.1: 155-182.
Attwood, Jayarava (2021). 'Revised Editions of the Prajñāpāramitāhṛdaya and the Xīnjīng.' Draft, July 2021.
Huifeng (2014). 'Apocryphal Treatment for Conze's Heart Problems: Non-attainment, Apprehension, and Mental Hanging in the Prajñāpāramitā Hṛdaya.' JOCBS 6: 72-105.
Kotyk, Jeffrey (2019). 'Chinese State and Buddhist Historical Sources on Xuanzang.' T'oung Pao 105(5-6): 513-544.
Ji, Yun (2017). 'Is the Heart Sūtra an Apocryphal Text? – A Re-examination.' Singapore Journal of Buddhist Studies 4: 9-113.

wu_zhi_yi_wu_de.issue: Should be 'wú dé wú xiàn guān 無得無現觀' (no attainment, no realization) as in Xuánzàng's T220
wu_zhi_yi_wu_de.source_error: Kumārajīva's T223 misread the Large Sutra passage

yi_wu_suo_de_gu.issue: Should close the previous sentence (negations), not open a new one. Represents anupalambhayogena ('through the practice of non-apprehension')

epithets.text: 是大神咒，是大明咒，是無上咒，是無等等咒
epithets.issue: Standard Prajñāpāramitā has three epithets (mahā-, anuttara-, asamasama-) representing vidyā. T251 adds fourth epithet 神咒 and shortens 明咒 to 咒 in two places. Kumārajīva's T223 has 是大明呪、無上明呪、無等等明呪
